commands.repair_reason: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the remaining blockers are exact-head proof and maintainer security/compatibility acceptance, not an isolated code repair.; Needs attention: The diff is security-sensitive and should not merge until current-head proof ties the OpenClaw change to an exact Codex runtime that delivers the required PreToolUse hook path. commands.review_summary:
The PR conditionally enables Codex unified exec for policy-active native PreToolUse relays, snapshots policy state into relay registration, refreshes Codex thread bindings when hook config changes, and keeps `openclaw hooks relay` stdout protocol-only.

PR surface: Source +79, Tests +265. Total +344 across 17 files.

Reproducibility: no. live current-main repro was run in this read-only review; source inspection and supplied PR proof identify the relay, unified-exec, and stdout-protocol paths involved.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the remaining blockers are exact-head proof and maintainer security/compatibility acceptance, not an isolated code repair.

target.title: fix(codex): restore native PreToolUse relay delivery
target.body:
## Summary

Narrow positive-delivery fix for Codex native `PreToolUse` relay enforcement.

This PR does **not** disable Codex native shell/file/MCP surfaces and does **not** apply the broader fail-closed fallback from #90633 / #90805. It keeps the native hook relay path and fixes the OpenClaw-side delivery conditions that made the locally patched Codex binary ineffective in the live OpenClaw runtime.

Changes:
- Emit Codex `features.unified_exec` plus legacy `experimental_use_unified_exec_tool` config when an active OpenClaw `PreToolUse` relay is installed, so shell-like native execution uses the hookable `exec_command` path instead of an unhooked local-shell response path.
- Snapshot whether OpenClaw `before_tool_call` / trusted tool policy was active at Codex run setup time, so the relay config cannot silently omit active PreToolUse when ambient policy state shifts later in the lifecycle.
- Refresh native hook thread binding when the relay config fingerprint changes, including `/btw` side-thread forks.
- Reserve `stdout` for protocol JSON on `openclaw hooks relay`; startup/Doctor diagnostics stay off stdout so Codex can parse the hook decision.
- Preserve permission-request-only relay behavior.

Important prerequisite: this depends on the local patched Codex 0.135 binary for openai/codex#23411. OpenClaw cannot make an unpatched Codex runtime emit the missing Code Mode `PreToolUse` event; this PR fixes the OpenClaw relay/config side once that Codex patch is installed.

## Root Cause

There were two separate gaps.

First, Codex `local_shell_call` response items do not traverse the client-side tool router where `PreToolUse` hooks run. The patched Codex binary can invoke `PreToolUse` for `exec_command` / code-mode exec, but OpenClaw still has to configure Codex onto the hookable unified exec path whenever OpenClaw depends on native `PreToolUse` delivery.

Second, after unified exec and trust binding were present, live OpenClaw still leaked the sentinel because `openclaw hooks relay` wrote startup/Doctor warnings to `stdout` before the hook JSON deny payload. Codex expects hook stdout to be parseable hook protocol JSON. With contaminated stdout, Codex ignored the denial and continued the command.

This PR addresses both OpenClaw-side pieces: install the hookable exec path when policy needs it, and keep relay stdout protocol-only.

## Real Behavior Proof

Exact head: `70a601da8d90b9da714b586a5a153b257c5e8398`

Local runtime conditions:
- Installed OpenClaw 2026.6.1 runtime roots were deployed from exact-head build output for proof.
- Installed `@openclaw/codex` runtime uses the locally patched Codex 0.135 binary for openai/codex#23411.
- Node v26.3.0 was used for the deployed runtime proof path.

Before this final stdout fix, exact head `76e7019684` still failed live gateway enforcement:

```text
test_firewall_blocks_codex_exec: FAIL
stdout: codex: SENTINEL CONTENTS REACHED OUTPUT (gateway/shell) - firewall did NOT block: ENFORCEMENT GAP
```

After `70a601da8d`, the same focused live gateway sentinel cell passed:

```text
test_firewall_blocks_codex_exec: PASS
stdout: codex: firewall blocked the sentinel read - enforcement verified (gateway/shell)
```

Direct runtime relay stdout proof:

```text
node .../openclaw.mjs hooks relay --provider codex --relay-id missing-relay --generation missing-generation --event pre_tool_use --timeout 1000

stdout: {"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"Native hook relay unavailable"}}
stderr: gateway/diagnostic messages only
```

Code Mode proof with patched Codex app-server + real deployed `openclaw.mjs hooks relay`:

```json
{
  "execMarkerExists": false,
  "requestCount": 2,
  "hookMethods": ["hook/started", "hook/completed"],
  "hookStatuses": ["running", "blocked"],
  "hookFeedback": ["Native hook relay unavailable"],
  "stderrContainsCodeModeExec": true,
  "stderrContainsBlocked": true
}
```

The corresponding Codex stderr included:

```text
Tool call blocked by PreToolUse hook: Native hook relay unavailable. Tool: code_mode_exec
```

Live OpenClaw agent proof with unified exec features enabled and a trusted relay showed the real firewall rule denied the sentinel read:

```text
Blocked as expected.
Result: OC Firewall denied the read with rule deny-fw-regression-sentinel-read, so the self-check passed.
```

## Validation

Exact head `70a601da8d90b9da714b586a5a153b257c5e8398`:

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/command-startup-policy.test.ts src/cli/command-path-policy.test.ts src/cli/native-hook-relay-cli.test.ts src/agents/harness/native-hook-relay.test.ts` PASS
- `git diff --check` PASS
- `env TMPDIR=/tmp/openclaw-codex-pretool-positive-narrow/.tmp OPENCLAW_BUILD_ALL_NO_PNPM=1 OPENCLAW_BUILD_VERBOSE=1 OPENCLAW_TSDOWN_HEARTBEAT_MS=10000 OPENCLAW_TSDOWN_TIMEOUT_MS=600000 nice -n 10 node scripts/build-all.mjs cliStartup` PASS
- Built worktree `openclaw.mjs hooks relay` stdout parseability check PASS
- Deployed runtime `openclaw.mjs hooks relay` stdout parseability check PASS
- Focused live regression `test_firewall_blocks_codex_exec` PASS after previously failing on `76e7019684`
- Code Mode app-server relay deny proof PASS: `hook/completed` status `blocked`, no command marker written, stderr names `Tool: code_mode_exec`

Notes:
- The exact-head commit was made with `--no-verify` because the local commit hook attempted `pnpm install` and aborted with `[ERR_PNPM_ABORTED_REMOVE_MODULES_DIR_NO_TTY]` in this symlinked-node_modules worktree. Focused tests and exact-head build passed after that.
- Broad CI-shaped remote validation still needs to run before this leaves Draft.

## Review Focus

Please review the OpenClaw-side delivery boundary:
- Is the unified exec enablement scoped correctly to active `PreToolUse` relay configs?
- Is the relay config fingerprint refresh sufficient to prevent stale app-server thread bindings?
- Is the `hooks relay` startup policy narrow enough while preserving protocol-only stdout?
- Does any remaining live proof need to be collected before marking ready for maintainer review?
